channel.title: doyouknow.app - English
channel.description: Surprising facts about UAE, Saudi Arabia, and the world.

item.title: Opening a Bank Account in Egypt: Guide
item.description: How foreign residents can open a bank account in Egypt: required documents, choosing a bank, account types, and practical tips for expats and long-term visitors.

item.title: Egypt Driving Guide: Rentals & Road Rules
item.description: Should you rent a car in Egypt? Car rental requirements, an International Driving Permit, road conditions, and why most visitors use drivers instead in 2026.

item.title: Nile Cruise Guide: Luxor to Aswan Trip Planning
item.description: How to plan a Nile cruise between Luxor and Aswan: cruise length options, what's included, choosing a ship, temple stops, and practical booking tips for 2026.

item.title: Cairo International Airport Guide
item.description: Navigating Cairo International Airport (CAI): terminals, visa on arrival, getting into the city, SIM cards, and practical tips for a smooth arrival in 2026.

item.title: Egypt Safety Guide for Travelers
item.description: Practical safety information for visiting Egypt in 2026: general crime, road safety, common tourist scams, health precautions, and staying informed while traveling.

item.title: Egypt Tipping & Baksheesh Guide
item.description: Understanding tipping in Egypt: what baksheesh means, typical amounts for restaurants, hotels, guides, and drivers, and practical tips for 2026.

item.title: Egypt Money & Currency Guide: ATMs & Cash
item.description: Managing money in Egypt: the Egyptian pound, ATM access, card acceptance, currency exchange, and practical budgeting tips for travelers in 2026.

item.title: Egypt SIM Card & Mobile Guide for Visitors
item.description: How to get a SIM card in Egypt: airport kiosks vs city shops, major carriers, tourist data plans, required documents, and practical tips for 2026.

item.title: Getting Around Egypt: Trains, Flights & Buses
item.description: How to travel between Egyptian cities: domestic flights, the Cairo–Luxor–Aswan train line, intercity buses, and how to choose the right option for 2026.

item.title: The History of Karaoke
item.description: Karaoke started with a Japanese musician who never patented his invention. Here is how singing along to instrumental tracks became global.

item.title: The History of Trading Card Games
item.description: From baseball cards to Magic: The Gathering, trading card games became a billion-dollar industry. Here is how the format actually started.

item.title: How Reading Aloud to Children Builds Literacy
item.description: Reading aloud to children is one of the most evidence-backed things parents can do for literacy. Here is what the research actually shows.

item.title: The Legend of Gilgamesh
item.description: The Epic of Gilgamesh is one of the oldest surviving works of literature. Here is the story of the Mesopotamian king who searched for eternal life.

item.title: The Story of Layla and Majnun
item.description: Layla and Majnun is one of the most famous love stories in Arabic literature. Here is the tragic tale behind the Middle East's Romeo and Juliet.

item.title: Understanding Empty Nest Syndrome
item.description: Empty nest syndrome is a real emotional adjustment, not just a phrase. Here is what happens when children leave home and how parents cope.

item.title: Understanding Herd Immunity
item.description: Herd immunity protects people who cannot be vaccinated, but only above a certain threshold. Here is how it actually works and why the threshold varies.

item.title: Understanding Perfectionism and How to Manage It
item.description: Perfectionism is not the same as having high standards. Here is the psychology behind it, why it can backfire, and how to manage it.

item.title: Understanding the Peter Principle
item.description: The Peter Principle explains why competent employees keep getting promoted until they fail. Here is the theory and how organizations try to avoid it.

item.title: Vitamins vs Minerals — What's the Difference
item.description: Vitamins and minerals both count as micronutrients, but they are chemically different and behave differently in the body. Here is what sets them apart.

item.title: What Is Bloom's Taxonomy
item.description: Bloom's Taxonomy is the framework behind how most modern lesson plans are built. Here are its six levels and how teachers actually use them.
